---
title: "Day 2 - Block 5"
subtitle: "Data Analysis & Coding 2"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D2 B5"
---

## Objective

- Add reliability controls to analysis agents
- Balance speed, cost, and reproducibility
- Stress one failure pathway

## Inputs

- Existing workflow from D2B4
- Reliability policy template
- Logging schema (stage/attempt/latency)

## Reliability control loop

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Run stage] --> B{Timeout?}
  B -->|No| C[Accept]
  B -->|Yes| D[Retry budget check]
  D -->|left| E[Backoff + rerun]
  E --> B
  D -->|empty| F[Fallback output]
  F --> G[Human review flag]
```

## Efficiency guardrails (from prior workshop)

**DO**
- Right-size models for task complexity
- Use efficient hardware and precision
- Prefer batching and caching when possible

**DON'T**
- Use largest model by default
- Use reasoning models for simple tasks
- Generate large outputs without purpose

## Batching and caching

- Batch non-urgent workloads
- Cache deterministic intermediate results
- Reuse embeddings/retrieval results across runs

## Hands-on

- Add timeout/retry/fallback policy
- Run one failure injection
- Capture logs: stage, attempt, latency, fallback reason

## Deliverable

- Reliability-enhanced workflow spec
- One injected failure test result
- One log excerpt with diagnosis

## Timebox

- 20 min lecture input
- 30 min group build
- 10 min debrief

## Submission link

- `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=MICRO_D2B5`

## Evaluation rubric

- Control-loop completeness
- Failure handling transparency
- Cost-speed tradeoff justification

## Extension task (optional)

- Add second fallback strategy
- Compare latency/cost outcomes

## Resource anchors

- R004, R018, R023
- R027, R028, R040

## Debrief

- What failed first?
- Which safeguard helped most?
- Which simplification gives biggest gain?
